diagrams.title: Kahn's Algorithm Step-by-Step Example
diagrams.description: Shows the execution of Kahn's algorithm on a sample DAG with vertices A,B,C,D,E and edges A→B, A→C, B→D, C→D, D→E
elements: Initial indegrees: A=0, B=1, C=1, D=2, E=1
Step 1: Queue[A], process A → result[A], B=0, C=0 → Queue[B,C]
Step 2: Queue[B,C], process B → result[A,B], D=1
Step 3: Queue[C], process C → result[A,B,C], D=0
Step 4: Queue[D], process D → result[A,B,C,D], E=0
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how Kahn's algorithm processes vertices in topological order using indegree counting

diagrams.title: DFS Finish Time Method
diagrams.description: Demonstrates DFS-based topological sort showing discovery times, exploration, and finish times
elements: DFS from A: discover A(1), discover B(2), discover D(3), finish D(4), finish B(5), discover C(6), finish C(7), finish A(8)
Stack after DFS (push on finish): D, B, C, A
Pop from stack: A, C, B, D - this is the topological order
diagrams.purpose: Understand how reverse finish times give topological ordering in DFS approach

diagrams.title: Cycle Detection in Kahn's Algorithm
diagrams.description: Shows how Kahn's algorithm fails when graph contains a cycle
elements: Graph with cycle: A→B, B→C, C→A
Queue is empty initially - cannot start
Algorithm terminates with 0 vertices processed, count=0 < V=3
diagrams.purpose: Illustrate cycle detection mechanism in Kahn's algorithm
